evals.name: transform-raw-idea
evals.prompt: I want to tweet about how most developers waste time optimizing code that doesn't matter. Can you make this into a viral tweet?
evals.expected_output: A tweet using contrarian or educational framework that challenges premature optimization, with analysis explaining the hook type, structure, and psychological triggers used

evals.name: improve-draft-tweet
evals.prompt: Here's my tweet draft: 'Starting a business is hard. You need to work hard and not give up and talk to customers. Also build good products.' Make this viral.
evals.expected_output: An improved version using personal story or listicle framework with specific details, numbers, or concrete advice. Should include analysis of what was changed and why it works better

evals.name: generate-from-topic
evals.prompt: Write a viral tweet about AI agents replacing jobs. I want to take a balanced take that's not too hype-y or too doom-y.
evals.expected_output: A tweet that uses contrarian or data-driven framework to stand out from typical AI hype, with analysis explaining how it avoids cliches while still being engaging

evals.name: thread-format
evals.prompt: I have a lot to say about why SaaS pricing is broken. Can you help me write this as a tweet thread? Main points: 1) free trials should not require credit card, 2) pricing pages hide real prices, 3) enterprise pricing is just a negotiation game
evals.expected_output: A multi-tweet thread with a strong hook tweet that stands alone, supporting tweets for each point, and a summary/CTA at the end. Each tweet should be skimmable and add value independently

evals.name: analyze-existing-viral
evals.prompt: This tweet got 10M views: 'I don't need a job. I need a reason to live.' Analyze why it went viral and then write me 3 similar-style tweets about work-life balance for software engineers.
evals.expected_output: Analysis of the original tweet's psychological triggers and structure, then 3 original tweets using similar frameworks adapted to the work-life balance topic for engineers
